title: Google I/O 2026: Everything Announced — Gemini Omni, Antigravity 2.0, Agentic Search, and More
subtitle: A live-updated roundup of every major announcement from today's Google I/O keynote as Sundar Pichai declares the start of the 'agentic Gemini era'
excerpt: Google I/O 2026 is underway. Here is a continuously updated breakdown of every product, model, and developer tool announced — from Gemini Omni's video generation to SynthID's new partners including OpenAI.
seo_title: Google I/O 2026: All Announcements — Gemini Omni, Spark, Antigravity, Search
seo_description: Complete roundup of everything Google announced at I/O 2026: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini Omni, Gemini Spark, Antigravity 2.0, agentic Search, SynthID expansion with OpenAI, and more.
body:
Last updated: May 19, 2026 — This article will be updated as announcements continue throughout the day.

Google's annual developer conference is live. CEO Sundar Pichai opened by declaring the company is now firmly in the "agentic Gemini era" — a shift from AI that answers questions to AI that takes action on your behalf. By the numbers: Google is now processing 3.2 quadrillion tokens per month across its surfaces, up 7x year-over-year. 8.5 million developers build with Google models monthly.

Models

Gemini 3.5 Flash — Available Now

The first release in the new 3.5 model family. Gemini 3.5 Flash beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on nearly all benchmarks, with the biggest gains in agentic coding. It outputs tokens four times faster than other frontier models while matching them on intelligence. Priced at less than half of comparable models. Available today across all Google products and the Gemini API. Gemini 3.5 Pro follows next month.

Gemini Omni Flash — Available Now

A brand new model family that can generate video output from any input modality. Described as combining Gemini's intelligence with Google's generative media models. Launching initially with video output; image and text outputs follow. Available today in the Gemini app, Google Flow, and YouTube Shorts. Developer API access rolling out in coming weeks.

Consumer Products

Gemini Spark — Beta Next Week

Google's 24/7 personal AI agent. Runs on dedicated cloud VMs, meaning it works without your device being active. Powered by Gemini 3.5 and the Antigravity harness. Integrates with Gmail, Calendar, Docs, Drive, and Tasks. MCP support for third-party tools coming soon. Chrome integration this summer. Trusted tester rollout starts this week; beta for Google AI Ultra subscribers ($200/month) opens next week in the U.S.

Gemini Spark Daily Brief

A second out-of-box agent announced alongside Spark. Daily Brief synthesizes your inbox, calendar, and tasks into a prioritized morning digest, complete with suggested next actions.

Gemini App Growth

The Gemini app now has 900 million monthly active users, up from 400 million at last year's I/O. Daily requests are up 7x in the same period. Over 50 billion images have been generated with Google's Nano Banana image generation models.

Search

Information Agents in Search

Personalized AI agents that run in the background 24/7 to surface relevant information and prompt action at exactly the right moment. Rolling out this summer to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers first.

Generative UI in Search — Free, This Summer

Search will use Gemini 3.5 Flash and Antigravity to build custom layouts and interactive visuals for individual queries. No subscription required — rolling out to all users this summer.

Persistent Dashboards in Search

For long-running research topics, Search will build custom dashboards and trackers — effectively mini apps generated on demand for specific tasks. Coming to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers in the U.S. first.

AI Mode Hits 1 Billion Users

AI Overviews now has 2.5 billion monthly active users. AI Mode, described as the biggest upgrade to Search ever, has already surpassed 1 billion monthly active users in just one year.

Developer Tools

Antigravity 2.0 — Available Today

Antigravity expands from a coding environment into a full platform for building and managing cohorts of autonomous agents. The new standalone desktop app acts as a central dashboard for agent interaction. Includes an optimized Flash variant that is 12x faster than other frontier models for agent-specific tasks. Available to Antigravity users today.

Managed Agents in Gemini API — Preview Today

Developers can now spin up a fully managed agent (the Antigravity agent) with a single API call. The agent gets an isolated Linux sandbox to reason, execute code, browse the web, and manage files. Custom agents can be defined with AGENTS.md and SKILL.md configuration files. Available in preview today; enterprise version on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform in private preview.

Google Workspace

Gmail Live

Voice-activated inbox search. Ask your inbox questions out loud — "What is my flight's gate number?" — and Gmail searches and synthesizes the answer instantly.

Docs Live

Conversational document creation. Dictate a "brain dump" and Docs Live organizes your thoughts, structures the document, and can pull relevant details from Gmail, Drive, Chat, and the web — with permission. Rolling out to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers and Workspace business customers this summer.

Keep Voice

Voice-to-organized-notes. Talk, and Keep converts your rambles into structured notes and lists automatically.

Google Pics — Trusted Testers Now, Summer Broadly

A new standalone image creation and editing tool built on the Nano Banana model. Treats every element as an individual object, enabling precise edits (swap objects, change colors, edit text in images, translate text) without regenerating the whole image. Integrates with Slides and Drive. Global rollout to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers this summer.

AI Inbox Expansion

Gmail's AI Inbox feature adds personalized draft replies, instant file access from email threads, and streamlined task management. Expanding from Ultra to all Google AI Plus and Pro subscribers in the U.S. starting today.

AI Safety and Transparency

SynthID Expands — OpenAI Now a Partner

Google's invisible AI watermarking technology has now marked over 100 billion images and videos, and 60,000 years of audio. Google is expanding SynthID watermark verification to Search and Chrome. New partners adopting SynthID: OpenAI, Kakao, and Eleven Labs. Content Credentials verification (showing whether content was AI-generated or camera-captured) is also coming to Search and Chrome.

Infrastructure

TPU 8th Generation

Announced at Cloud Next, the 8th gen TPU takes a dual-chip approach: TPU 8t (training, 3x raw compute of prior gen, scales across 1M+ TPUs globally) and TPU 8i (inference-optimized, dramatically lower latency). Both deliver up to 2x better performance-per-watt. Google expects to spend approximately $180–190 billion in capex in 2026.

Google Labs Experiments

Project Genie + Street View

Google's generative world model Genie can now be grounded in real-world Street View imagery. Users can explore and reimagine any location in the U.S. with the selected style ("Ocean World," "Desert Sands," etc.). Available now to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally via labs.google/projectgenie.

Gemini for Science

A collection of AI tools for scientific research, including Science Skills — a connector between agentic platforms like Antigravity and over 30 major life science databases. Available today on GitHub and in Antigravity.

Google Flow Updates

Flow gets a new planning and reasoning agent for complex creative projects, plus Gemini Omni integration for video. Users can vibe-code creative tools directly inside Flow.

Stitch Updates

Google's design tool gets a new Stitch Agent for real-time collaborative design — described as a "live, collaborative partnership" for UI creation.
